title: cremanthodium ellisii var. ramosum
intro: Cremanthodium ellisii var. ramosum, also known as the Cremanthodium ramosum, is a rare and unique flowering plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is native to the high-altitude meadows and rocky outcrops of the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves are used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
